item.title: The planes of September 11, 2001
item.description:
On September 11, 2001, four airliners were hijacked by terrorists with the intent to turn them into weapons. The original plan by Khalid Sheikh Mohammed called for 10 planes to be hijacked and flown into various targets such the World Trade Center, Empire State Building, The Pentagon, The Capitol, The White House, the US Bank Tower in LA, Sears/Willis Tower in Chicago, Columbia Center in Seattle, and others. However, this plan was rejected by Bin Laden for being too difficult to pull off.
American Airlines Flight 11 aircraft was a Boeing 767-223ER delivered in 1987, registration number N334AA. The capacity of the aircraft was 158 passengers, but the September 11 flight carried 81 passengers and 11 crew members. Flight 11 was scheduled to depart at 7:45am and began it's takeoff run at 7:59am and travel to Los Angeles International Airport. (Picture is N334AA, the aircraft flying Flight 11, taxiing at Manchester Airport on April 8, 2001, five months before the attacks.)
11 crew members were Captain John Ogonowski, First Officer Thomas McGuinness, and flight attendants Barbara Arestegui, Jeffrey Collman, Sara Low, Karen Martin, Kathleen Nicosia, Betty Ong, Jean Roger, Dianne Snyder, and Amy Sweeney. TV Producer David Angel (Fraiser, Cheers, etc) was on the flight with his wife Lynn. Actress Berry Berenson, the widow of Anthony Perkins (from the movie 'Psycho') was also on the flight. Family Guy creator Seth Macfarland was supposed to be on the flight, but arrived late to the airport and missed the flight (his agent told him the plane leaves at 8:15am so he arrived at 7:50 as the plane was taxing onto the runway)
The hijackers of Flight 11 were led by Mohammad Atta who would pilot the plane after the other 'muscle hijackers' took over the plane. (on a related note, it's been reported in the 9/11 Commission that the muscle hijackers on the planes were unware they were on suicide missions so that they wouldn't back out at last minute) 15 minutes after takeoff, the hijackers took control of the plane. About five minutes later, the plane turned south and started following the Hudson river down to New York City.
Flight 11 flight attendants Amy Sweeney and Betty Ong were able to call the ground and relay important information, such as who the hijackers were, what seats they were in, where they were heading, and more. Both were on the phone with officials when the plane crashed. Towards the end of her call, Amy Sweeney said "oh my God. I see buildings. Water. We are too low. We are too low. Oh my God!" followed by a burst of loud static. Three minutes later CNN reported that a plane had hit the WTC. Part of Betty Ong's phone call after the plane was hijacked: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AUpG5CMbQRw
At 8:46am, Flight 11 crashed into the North Tower of the World Trade Center, hitting at 480mph. Everyone on the plane and people on the impacted floors were killed instantly.
Flight 11 hit the North Tower dead on, which destroyed the center staircases and elevators. This essentially doomed the 1000+ people who were trapped above the impact zone (most were attending a technology conference at Windows of the World on the top floors of the North Tower when the plane hit)
These photos were taken moments after impact
After the impact, various debris littered the streets below. Seat cushions, pieces of fuselage, part of a plane engine, body parts, etc. Here, a part of the landing gear lays several streets away. An hour and a half later, the North Tower would collapse due to stress from the impact and heat from the fires.
United Airlines Flight 175 was a Boeing 767-222 (registration number N612UA). It had a capacity of 168 passengers and crew, but on September 11 the flight carried 56 passengers and 9 crew members. Flight 175 was scheduled to depart at 8:00am for Los Angeles International Airport and took off at 8:14am (picture is N612UA, the hijacked aircraft, taxiing at San Francisco International Airport in 1999.)
The nine crew members included Captain Victor Saracini, First Officer Michael Horrocks, and flight attendants Robert Fangman, Amy Jarret, Amy King, Kathryn Laborie, Alfred Marchand, Michael Tarrou, and Alicia Titus Excluding the hijackers, the passengers on the flight included 35 men, 12 women, and three children who were all under the age of 5
The Hijackers were led by Marwan al-Shehhi who would pilot the plane after the other hijackers took control over the plane. The hijackers took control of the plane at about the same time as Flight 11 crashed into the WTC. Shortly after 8:46am, the plane started a rapid ascent and climbed dramatically. The plane narrowly missed several other planes (even missing a Tampa-bound Delta flight by nearly 200ft) that were flying nearby as it climbed to 30,000 feet.
Peter Hanson was taking his wife Sue and 2 year old daughter Christine on a business trip to California followed by a surprise vacation for Christine to Disneyland. It would also be Christine's first plane ride. He called his parents from back of plane. He said plane was very shaky and making hard climbs and drops and that passengers were vomiting in the aisles. He believed the terrorists would crash the plane into building in Chicago, but assured his dad "don't worry, if it happens, it will be very fast". At the end of the phone call, he said "oh my God" several times as a woman in the background screamed. The phone went dead as his parents saw Flight 175 hit on live TV. Flight attendant Robert Fangman called United Airlines and told them the plane was hijacked and that both pilots were dead and that a flight attendant had been stabbed.
In the final five minutes of Flight 175, the plane dropped from 30,000 ft before impact meaning the plane was power-diving at almost 6000ft a minute before impact. The final moments of the plane watched by people on TV and those in NY show the plane banking hard before the crash. Hijacker pilot Marwan was banking the plane to make sure he hit the tower instead of just clipping it with his wing. Due to the way the plane was turning, anyone on the left side of the plane would have seen the WTC fast approaching (with one tower already burning) until the final moment
At 9:03am, Flight 175 crashed into the South Tower of the World Trade Center at 580mph. Everyone onboard the plane was killed instantly, along with many in the impacted floors (although a few people survived the impacted floors and escaped the building.
Because of the way the plane was banked, the crash took out the majority of the central staircases and elevators in the South Tower. However, there was one staircase that remained because it was not hit directly by the plane and several dozen people were able to escape from the upper floors and out of the building. However, most people trapped above the impact zone were either told to stay where they are (because of the result of the trampling that occured during the 1993 bombing) or because smoke was rising up the tower and people ascended up the tower to escape the smoke.
Like the previous plane, debris of Flight 175 littered the streets below. Here is part of one of the engines found several blocks away. Less than an hour later, the South Tower will collapse.
American Airlines Flight 77 aircraft was a Boeing 757-223 (registration number N644AA) The capacity of the aircraft was 188 passengers, but with 58 passengers on September 11, the load factor was 33 percent. American Airlines said that Tuesdays were the least-traveled day of the week, with the same load factor seen on Tuesdays in the previous three months for Flight 77. Flight 77 was scheduled to depart Washington Dulles International Airport at 8:10am and took off at 8:20am bound for Los Angeles International Airport (picture is N644AA, the aircraft used for Flight 77, in March 1995)
The flight crew included pilot Charles Burlingame, First Officer David Charlebois, and flight attendants Michele Heidenberger, Jennifer Lewis, Kenneth Lewis, and Renee May. Excluding the hijackers, of the 59 other passengers and crew on board, there were 26 men, 22 women, and five children ranging in age from three to eleven (several of the children were 6th graders traveling with their teachers to California for a field trip sponsored by National Geographic which also had several employees on the plane. Georgetown University coach John Thompson booked a ticket for Flight 77 but changed plans at the last minute to attend a talk show. He felt the impact of the crash from his home in Washington DC. https://i.imgur.com/v8WD8nf.jpeg (Photo of American Airlines Flight 77 the morning of September 11. A teacher who was dropping off the students for the field trip took this photo of the plane as people were boarding)
The five hijackers were led by Hani Hanjour who would pilot the plane after the muscle hijackers took control of the aircraft. It is believed the hijacking took place around 8:50am (shortly after the first plane hit the WTC). At 8:54am, the plane started to make a U-turn and started flying back towards Washington DC.
TV commentator Barbara Olson was supposed to fly out the previous day, but went on Flight 77 on 9/11 so she could have breakfast with her husband (it was his birthday that day). She called her husband twice, saying that the plane was hijacked and that passengers and crew were huddled in the back. Her husband told her of the attacks on the WTC and Barbara knew how bleak the situation was. She asked her husband if there was anything she could do to help before the phone cut off. Flight 77 crashed into the Pentagon less than 10 minutes later. Flight Attendant Renee May called her mother and told her the plane was being hijacked and that the passengers and staff had been moved to the back of the plane and told her mother to call American Airlines.
During the last few minutes of the flight, hijacker pilot Hani apparently missed the Pentagon (he was flying too high) and had the plane make a sharp 330-degree descending loop back to the Pentagon (https://i.imgur.com/Uzre2YJ.jpeg). At the end of the loop at 2000ft, he advanced the throttles to maximum power and dived the plane into the Pentagon at 530mph. Everyone on the plane was killed instantly, along with many Pentagon workers in the impact zone.
Traveling at 530mph, the plane passed over the nearby freeway (clipping several lightpoles along the way, one which fell onto a taxi as the plane screamed overhead). One witness was walking down the sidewalk next to the freeway when the plane shot overhead. He said as the plane passed he could see someone looking out one of the windows but the plane was going too fast too see if it was a man or woman. As the plane banked slightly to the left, the left engine hit the ground and the right engine hit a power generator.
As the plane hit the building, it fragmented into small pieces which shot through the building along with the fireball from the ignited fuel. The crash took out a large section of Army administrative offices and the Naval Operations Center. As it crashed into the Pentagon, the debris shot 310 feet into the Pentagon within 8/10s of a second before punching out through the C-Ring in the middle of the building: https://i.imgur.com/0QAqfOM.jpeg
Various debris from the plane was found all over the lawn in front of the Pentagon along with in the building. Recovery efforts found both engines, landing gear, parts of fuselage, the black box, and remains from passengers.
United 93 was a Boeing 757–222, registration number N591UA. The airplane had a capacity of 182 passengers; the September 11 flight carried 37 passengers and seven crew. United 93 departed Newark International Airport at 8:42am (four minutes before American Flight 11 crashed into the World Trade Center about 10 miles to the north east in Manhattan) and was flying to San Francisco International Airport. (picture is N591UA taxiing on September 8, 2001, three days before it was hijacked.)
Flight 93 was carrying 37 passengers and 7 crew members. The seven crew members were Captain Jason Dahl, First Officer LeRoy Homer, Jr., and flight attendants Lorraine Bay, Sandra Bradshaw, Wanda Green, CeeCee Lyles and Deborah Welsh. Actor Mark Wahlberg was scheduled to fly out to California with some friends on Flight 93 but changed plans at the last minute to attend a concert.
Unlike the other planes, Flight 93 had four hijackers (as opposed to the five hijackers found on the other planes). It is now believed Mohammed al-Qahtani was supposed to be the 20th hijacker but was denied entry into the United States in August 2001 when he arrived in Orlando with a one-way ticket, several thousand dollars cash, and couldn't speak any English (therefore believed to be an illegal immigrant and was sent back) The hijacker pilot was Ziad Jarrah while the others were the muscle hijackers. Hijackers took control of the plane at 9:28am and from there made a turn and started heading to Washington DC. It is believed the plane's target was the Capitol Building in Washington DC.
Jeremy Glick (far left) called his wife saying that the plane was hijacked by Iranian-looking men in red bandanas. He said that he and some other passengers were going to fight back the hijackers, telling his wife that some of them looked really small and that he could take them (Jeremy had martial art training). Todd Beamer (middle left) tried to use a credit card to place a call, but the call was routed to a customer service representative. He talked with GTE supervisor Lisa Jefferson about what happened and that he and other passengers will try to take back the plane. His last audible words in the background of the call "are you ready, let's roll". Tom Burnett (middle right) and his wife exchanged phone calls several times, with his wife alerting him about the attacks on the WTC and the Pentagon. Knowing the plane is on a suicide mission, he said that he and some of the other passengers would try to take back the plane. His last words to her were "don't worry, we're going to do something". Mark Bingham (far right) called his mother and told her the plane was hijacked by some men who appeared to have a bomb and that he and some other guys were going to try and take back the plane. Flight Attendant CeeCee Lyles left this message for her husband about 10 minutes before the plane crashed: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fUrxsrTKHN4
The passengers revolted against the terrorists starting around 9:57am. Pilot Ziad started shaking the plane and rolling the plane in order to slow down the passenger revolt. The blackbox recorded sounds of glass breaking and stuff being smashed along with lots of yelling. At 10:03am, the plane rolled on it's right and plummeted to the ground, nosediving inverted into the ground at 563mph, creating seismic activity in the area. Everyone onboard was killed instantly.
Val McClatchey was watching news coverage of the attacks when the plane screamed overhead. She went to grab her camera and as she did the plane crashed, creating a loud explosion which shattered windows and shook things from walls. She ran outside and took a picture of the mushroom cloud rising up nearby.
First responders arrived at the scene at 10:06am. Since the plane nosedived into the ground at well over 500mph, there was a massive smoldering crater in the ground 10ft deep and 50ft wide. Nearby trees were on fire from the fireball of the crash.
At about 10:30am, CNN and other media channels were reporting that a large plane had crashed in Pennsylvania.
Of all the planes that crashed, United 93 had the most wreckage recovered. Various debris was found all over the area and buried in the earth (one of the engines was found 20ft below the surface) at the impact site. Rescue workers found remains from all passengers but only about 8% of the total mass, the rest was consumed in the crash or buried in the earth at the impact site.
Within hours of the event, the news began to report which airlines and flights were involved https://i.imgur.com/yYAk8UI.jpeg By the end of the day, air traffic in the United States was shut down and the investigation into those responsible began.
Standing in the footprints of the original World Trade Center are two reflecting pools, each with all the names of the victims of the September 11 attacks and the 1993 WTC bombing.
Located outside where the impact site was, the Pentagon Memorial features 184 benches to memorialize the 184 victims of the Pentagon attack. If the bench is facing away from the building, that bench has the name of someone who was killed in the Pentagon. If the bench is facing towards the building, that bench has the name of someone who was on Flight 77. On the Pentagon itself at the exact place of impact is one of the original bricks that is scorched from the fire.
Located in Shanksville, Pennsylvania is the Flight 93 memorial. There is a large memorial wall featuring names of all the victims from the plane. There is a special path that leads to the crash site (a large boulder marks the position) for family members of victims to visit: https://i.imgur.com/keAxA1l.jpeg
item.dc:creator: W0LFPAW

item.title: we engage in a bit of tomfoolery
item.dc:creator: worksafe

item.title: Twenty Years Later - The best speech about 9/11 came from Jon Stewart nine days after it happened
item.description:
Jon Stewart returned to The Daily Show weeks after the 9/11 attacks, and like many comedians in the moment, he made a point of getting real with his audience about the terror and devastation that America was facing.
Stewart’s immediate response to 9/11 was an incredibly moving one. The show, filmed in New York City, returned to airwaves nine days after the deadliest terror attack ever on American soil.
Instead of opening the talk show with a number of jokes, Stewart delivered an incredibly powerful message that started with an important question to his viewers: "Are you OK?"
Stewart periodically got choked up and admitted he was terrified, but inspired viewers with his message of strength, resilience and spirit, as well as is empathy and compassion.
In his monologue, Stewart noted that he would “grieve” but not “despair,” and invoked one of his earliest memories—the assassination of Dr. Martin Luther King, and how the country came back from that horrific incident and praised first responders.
September 11 changed the tone of the show, and that's something Stewart was immediately aware of. Listening to JS' monologue is powerful regardless of the day or the year, but it feels especially pressing again on September 11, in 2021.
Thanks to the following resources for helping me put this together: https://www.newsweek.com/jon-stewarts-9-11-monologue-seems-get-more-powerful-each-viewing-1531320 https://parade.com/1087123/jessicasager/jon-stewart-9-11/ https://commons.lib.jmu.edu/cgi/viewcontent.cgi?article=1058&c
item.dc:creator: inkican

item.title: It's my fault. Taken in by a sob story.
item.description: It sounded legitimate. Person had been on Imgur for some months, posted quite a few times, showed her cat, was in a difficult situation and needed funds. I decided to donate. Received no thanks nor even acknowledgement of receipt. That might be excusable...but then I got additional requests from them. Then several requests from other people unknown to me. Won't happen again. I'm sorry for those who are genuinely in financial difficulties, but there's the old adage: Fool me once, shame on you. Fool me twice, shame on me. Scammers are ruining the internet.
item.dc:creator: prfesser

item.title: British actor Will Mellor remembers a teacher who believed in him
item.dc:creator: hmo786

item.title: Actual convo with co-worker
item.description: I can't wait to quit this job full of conservative nutbunnies. This wasn't really a text convo, it was an in-person, I just figured it would be better represented as text. She then went on to complain about how Trump got fact-checked and Harris didn't, and I explained that Trump got fact-checked on actual, genuine, obviously verifiable lies and she went on and on about how they should have fact-checked the 'Charlottsville' comment. Freaking really?
item.dc:creator: OohDaLolly

item.title: German Foreign Office surprise with sense of humor, humiliating Trump (if even possible)
item.description: In his closing remarks, Trump slammed my country, Germany, claiming we regret our decision to transition to renewable energy. “You believe in things like we’re not going to frack, we’re not going to take fossil fuel, we’re not going to do things that are going to be strong, whether you like it or not,” he said, addressing Harris. “Germany tried that, and within one year, they were back to building normal energy plants,” he added. My take on that: We tried, we evolved, and we are running more and more on green energy. It is expensive, it is a huge task, but it is possible. On the other hand, I had Dogwurst with Catkraut and can´t get off it!
item.dc:creator: amalfomat

item.title: I won the world's Stone Skimming Championship
item.description:
I travelled to Easdale Island in Scotland over the weekend where I took part in the world's Stone Skimming Championship. I entered with the idea of having some fun and getting to meet people. After seeing many people warm up I thought to myself I haven't a hope of making the final. Over 350 people took part in the competition and 15 made the final. https://youtube.com/shorts/MzUFjoRv2mk?si=EP6Bpr9fh8F-K9hp
Stones thrown have to be from the island and must measure no more the 3 inches in diameter. In the final you have 3 throws and your distance is combined from those 3 shots, your shots must stay inside the barriers also. Me and one other contestant both had the same score after 3 throws so we had a signal stone throw down, the rest is history.
This is a small clip of a practice shot from the morning of. I'm the 1st Irish person to win the competition and it just goes to show that if you're willing to give things a try you may be surprised with the outcome. This was my 1st competition and I came away the trophy and new friends. I've copied a link above to a short video of the day.
item.dc:creator: Masterleftspud

item.title: Sizzle real from last night’s presidential debate. Pease, PLEASE vote!
item.description: I had to break up to fit IMGUR’s time format, see original here: https://x.com/kamalahq/status/1833726104428826665 In 2016 Clinton looked great in the presidential debates, but she lost the election partially because people assumed she would win, and didn’t go to vote. Please don’t let history repeat itself! Vote! Register to vote and/or check the status of your voter registration by going here: IWillVote.com or to vote.gov Certain states continue to accidentally purge legally registered voters from their rolls, so check to make sure you haven’t been purged. Be sure to vote in every election, including the “small” ones. The “big” politicians often got their start by winning the small elections. You can help weed out the bad ones early. #vote Edit: If you make use of mail-in voting, check trackvote.org to find out if your state offers a ballot tracking service. Hundreds of thousands of mail-in votes do not get counted due to errors made by votes (like not signing the envelope correctly, et. al.), you may be able to check to make sure your vote is counted correctly and notified if it's not, so you can submit a corrected ballot in time.

item.description:
Guinness lamb stew
Lentil soup
Irish breakfast
Khachapuri
Steak with mandioca
Tapioca pancake
Irish stew
Oysters
seafood pie
Fish and chips
Clam chowder
Spinach and rolled eggplant
item.dc:creator: Gigi95

item.title: War and Peas is a teenager now!
item.description:
We cannot believe it, but we've been making War and Peas for 13 years now!
In that time we have created hundreds (or thousands even?) of comic strips and while we sometimes feel like old hands, we still always feel a tingling sensation when we post a new comic.
“Funnies” sometimes seem detached and yet are always highly personal. Although these stories (or at least most of them) have not happened to us personally, they flow out of us onto the blank canvas and create something akin to meaning.
Thanks to our readers (YOU) we can continue this path and we couldn't be more grateful to be on this journey with you.
Forever yours, Elizabeth & Jonathan
item.dc:creator: Warandpeasofficial

item.title: Relativity and Non-Euclidean Geometry
item.description: This video is interesting but also has me posing a question: "Exactly how is space and time intersecting physically to cause this warping. Also what details explain this warping effect outside of repeating traditional arguments?"
item.dc:creator: RElGNMAN
